title: On certain C0 -aspects of contactomorphism groups
speaker: Vukašin Stojisavljević
affiliation: Centre de recherches mathématiques, University of Montreal
abstract:
We will explore certain C0-rigidity and flexibility phenomena in the study of contact transformations. In particular, we will show how the dichotomy between contact squeezing and non-squeezing is related to the Rokhlin property of the group of contact homeomorphisms. Assuming a more quantitative viewpoint, we will define new distances on the group of contact homeomorphisms and show that, in some cases, they satisfy a form of C0
-stability. A technical result behind this stability is C0
-continuity of Sandon's spectral invariants. The talk is based on a joint work with Baptiste Serraille.

proof_stages.label: Contact squeezing versus contact non-squeezing
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls symplectic non-squeezing, contrasts it with elementary squeezing in standard contact space, and then introduces the isotopic contact non-squeezing phenomenon on R^{2n} x S^1.

proof_stages.label: Contact isotopies and C0 contact homeomorphism groups
proof_stages.summary: Contact Hamiltonians are used to parametrize contact isotopies, and the C0 closures of compactly supported contactomorphism groups on R^{2n+1} and R^{2n} x S^1 are fixed as the main groups.

proof_stages.label: Rokhlin property as a conjugacy-flexibility test
proof_stages.summary: The Rokhlin property is defined as existence of a dense conjugacy class, and standard examples show that continuous conjugacy invariants obstruct it while flexible homeomorphism groups may have it.

proof_stages.label: Rokhlin and non-Rokhlin theorem from squeezing
proof_stages.summary: For R^{2n+1}, a dense sequence of elements is squeezed into disjoint shrinking balls and packed into one map with dense conjugacy class, while on R^{2n} x S^1 non-squeezing contradicts the existence of such a dense conjugacy class.

proof_stages.label: Sandon spectral norm and C0 local boundedness
proof_stages.summary: After noting that genuine continuous conjugation-invariant norms are impossible, the talk recalls Sandon's integer-valued spectral norm via translated points and proves the new C0-local-boundedness and lower-semicontinuity properties needed to extend it to contact homeomorphisms.

proof_stages.label: Applications to conjugacy classes and fragmentation
proof_stages.summary: The locally bounded extended norm bounds how closures of conjugacy classes can intersect, gives a norm-based proof of non-Rokhlin behavior, and yields lower bounds and unboundedness results for contact fragmentation and quantitative conjugacy distances.
